phrases.intent_en: Ask what time the market opens
phrases.text_local: Le marché ouvre à quelle heure ?
phrases.pronunciation: luh mar-SHAY oov-ruh ah kel UHR
phrases.cultural_note: The Place Richelme market runs every morning except Monday, from around 7h to 13h. The Saturday market is the largest and spills into neighbouring squares. Arrive early for the best produce selection.

phrases.intent_en: Ask for a taste of the Provençal cheese
phrases.text_local: Puis-je goûter ce fromage de Provence, s'il vous plaît ?
phrases.pronunciation: pwee-ZHUH goo-TAY suh fro-MAHZH duh proh-VAHNS, seel voo PLEH
phrases.cultural_note: It is perfectly normal to ask for a taste (une dégustation) at cheese and charcuterie stalls. Look for Banon — a small goat's cheese wrapped in chestnut leaves — a prized Provençal AOC speciality.

phrases.intent_en: Ask the price of a bunch of lavender
phrases.text_local: C'est combien ce bouquet de lavande ?
phrases.pronunciation: seh kohm-BYEHN suh boo-KEH duh lah-VAHND
phrases.cultural_note: Dried lavender from the Plateau de Valensole is a signature Provençal souvenir. The bunches at the market are generally fresher and less expensive than in tourist shops. The scent is strongest just after harvest in July.

phrases.intent_en: Ask for the freshest seasonal vegetables
phrases.text_local: Quels légumes sont les plus frais en ce moment ?
phrases.pronunciation: kel lay-GOOM sohn lay ploo FREH ahn suh moh-MAHN
phrases.cultural_note: The market is strictly seasonal. In summer expect tomatoes, courgettes, peppers, and aubergines for ratatouille. In winter look for artichokes, fennel, and the prized black truffle in season from December to February.

phrases.intent_en: Ask for a bag to carry the purchases
phrases.text_local: Avez-vous un sac, s'il vous plaît ?
phrases.pronunciation: ah-VAY voo uhn SAK, seel voo PLEH
phrases.cultural_note: Many Provençal locals shop daily and bring their own basket (un panier). Plastic bags have been banned in French markets since 2017. Some vendors sell charming woven market baskets alongside their produce.
